message:
fix(db/pool): coalesce back-to-back archive-salvage reconstructs (#105)

Issue #105 reported two \`database reconstruction from archive
complete\` log lines ≈10 ms apart with identical counters. The root
cause was two query-path requests detecting the corrupt verdict in
the same millisecond — both took the recovery path, both issued
full archive-salvage reconstructs, and both rewrote the live DB back
to the same healthy state. Archive salvage costs O(100 ms) per MB
of archive, so a second concurrent rebuild right after a successful
first one is pure wasted work; the second caller's inputs are
identical and the first has already delivered a healthy primary.

Introduce a 10 s per-primary-path coalescing window. Within the
window, a successful prior reconstruct's stats are returned verbatim
to the next caller instead of re-running the rebuild.

Changes in \`reconstruct_sqlite_file_with_archive_salvage\`:

- New private \`RECONSTRUCT_COALESCE_WINDOW: Duration =
  Duration::from_secs(10)\` constant. Ten seconds is long enough to
  absorb the worst-case observed window between concurrent corrupt-
  verdict readers (~O(ms)) plus safety margin, and short enough
  that if the DB re-corrupts after a reconstruct — which sets the
  verdict back to \`broken\` via a normal integrity check failure —
  the next reconstruct call lands after the window expires
  naturally. Production code never needs to invalidate the cache
  manually.

- Process-wide \`RECENT_RECONSTRUCT_CACHE: OnceLock<Mutex<HashMap<
  PathBuf, (Instant, ReconstructStats)>>>\` lazily initialized via
  \`recent_reconstruct_cache()\`. Keyed on \`primary_path\` so
  reconstructs on different mailboxes stay independent.

- Helpers \`recent_reconstruct_lookup(path, now) -> Option<(age,
  stats)>\` and \`recent_reconstruct_store(path, now, stats)\` each
  eagerly \`retain\`-evict stale entries whose age exceeds the
  window before any lookup or store, so the map can't accumulate
  entries for mailboxes that never reconstruct again.

- Poisoned-mutex handling via \`PoisonError::into_inner\` — a
  poisoned cache must not abort a reconstruct; we accept whatever
  state the prior panicker left behind and carry on.

Public function body:

- Before calling \`with_recovery_admission\`, probe the cache with
  \`recent_reconstruct_lookup(primary_path, now)\`. On hit, log an
  \`info\` line with the path, age_ms, and the coalesced stats,
  then return the cached stats immediately. No admission semaphore
  is touched on the coalesced path.
- On miss, run the normal admission-controlled reconstruct, and on
  \`Ok(stats)\` store \`(now, stats)\` under the primary_path so
  the next caller inside the window coalesces.

Test-only: \`reset_recent_reconstruct_cache_for_test()\` behind
\`#[cfg(test)]\` clears the cache between tests. Production code
never calls this (re-corruption naturally re-opens the path).

Regression tests added under \`mod tests\`:

- \`recent_reconstruct_cache_returns_fresh_entry_within_window\`
  stores stats \`{projects: 10, messages: 342}\`, looks them up
  10 ms later, asserts the counters are preserved and the
  reported \`age\` is ≤ 10 ms. Reset at start and end to keep
  other tests clean.
- \`recent_reconstruct_cache_evicts_past_window\` stores an entry,
  advances 10 s + 1 ms via \`Instant\` arithmetic, and asserts
  the lookup misses. Proves stale entries don't leak a stale
  stats payload into a genuinely new reconstruct.
- \`recent_reconstruct_cache_is_keyed_per_path\` stores under
  path A, asserts the lookup succeeds under A and misses under
  path B. Proves the coalescing is per-mailbox — one mailbox's
  reconstruct never satisfies another's verdict.

Pure race-condition fix. No API or serialization change and no
effect on mailboxes that have not recently reconstructed.
